title: Registry Publication, SBOM, Provenance, Vulnerabilities, Policies, and CI Release · Release Workshop
summary: A platform pairing session must produce the first working signed and policy-gated image release packet for registry publication, sbom, provenance, vulnerabilities, policies, and ci release, with predictions recorded before each simulated build or lifecycle change. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of signed and policy-gated image release packet while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Publish least-privilege authenticated content, verify digest after push, handle immutable tags, retention, mirrors, rate limits, and failure without credential leakage.
Generate, attach, inspect, export, validate, and interpret SPDX SBOM attestations including build-stage and final-image coverage.
Produce minimal or maximal SLSA provenance, locate materials and parameters, protect secret values, and verify the statement against source and builder policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Ci Release Rollback
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-ci-release-rollback-75vnsm". Tailor its release-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Ci Release Rollback.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Ci Release Rollback into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Ci Release Rollback, keep Docker Ci Release Rollback connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-ci-release-rollback-75vnsm
  - competency: docker-ci-release-rollback
    artifact: release-policy.yaml
    decision: ci release rollback
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: multi-tenant build farm using an isolated BuildKit driver
    fault-injection: deny outbound DNS after initial service discovery
    retained-proof: effective user capability filesystem and network inspection
    case-key: 75vnsm
    change: |
      source: reviewed-commit
      base: approved-digest
      output: immutable-registry-digest
      sbom: spdx-attestation
      provenance: max
      vulnerability-policy: severity-and-exploitability
      signature: identity-bound
    verify: docker buildx imagetools inspect candidate@sha256:release && docker scout cves candidate@sha256:release
    failure: Reject tag-only promotion, SBOM-as-vulnerability-scan, unsigned attestations, ignored base drift, or severity alone without reachability and exception ownership.
    repair: Bind source, builder, inputs, output digest, SBOM, provenance, signature, scan policy, exception expiry, and promotion evidence.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Gate tests, builds, policies, multi-platform manifests, signatures, attestations, promotion, deployment digest, rollback, and post-release verification in CI.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Docker Ci Release Rollback, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Docker Ci Release Rollback precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop Docker Ci Release Rollback, trace this concrete input and predict build, image, container, mount, network, Compose, policy, and teardown evidence before reviewing it: A workshop candidate has a mutable tag, incomplete SBOM, minimal provenance, critical scanner finding, and unsigned exception. Bind source, base, builder, attestations, signature, policy, and promotion to a digest. Competency probe: Gate tests, builds, policies, multi-platform manifests, signatures, attestations, promotion, deployment digest, rollback, and post-release verification in CI.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Docker Ci Release Rollback, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: Retagging latest is sufficient promotion and rollback evidence for a production release.
